workflow_rationale: This is a focused reliability improvement in a single codebase. All changes are related to crash safety and recovery, with clear dependencies between subtasks.

phases.name: Crash Safety Implementation
phases.description: Make file writes atomic and add recovery mechanisms

subtasks.description: Make write_meta atomic with temp+rename pattern
subtasks.notes: Implemented using tempfile::Builder to create temp file in same directory as target, write JSON to it, then atomically persist() to final path. Also added tempfile to bop-core dependencies. All 96 tests pass.

subtasks.description: Make write_providers atomic with temp+rename pattern
subtasks.notes: Implemented atomic write pattern: write to providers.json.tmp, then atomically rename to providers.json. All 20 provider tests pass.

subtasks.description: Add recover_orphans function with corrupt meta.json handling
subtasks.notes: Implemented recover_orphans function that: (1) scans running/ for orphaned cards, (2) checks PID liveness via read_pid/is_alive, (3) creates minimal recovery meta.json (id + stage=pending) when meta is corrupt/missing, (4) moves all orphans to pending/ (no retry logic, simpler than reap_orphans). Added 6 comprehensive tests covering dead PID, corrupt meta.json, missing meta.json, live PID skip, empty dir, and no PID file cases. All tests pass. Returns Vec<String> of recovered card IDs.

verification.instructions: Review code to confirm recover_orphans is called after paths::ensure_cards_layout and before the dispatch loop begins

subtasks.notes: Added reaper::recover_orphans call after directory setup and before filesystem watcher. Call is placed at line 48, after paths::ensure_cards_layout and before the dispatch loop. Logs recovered cards with eprintln if any are found. Verified placement is correct: after directory paths are defined, after lock acquisition, before watcher setup, and before main dispatch loop.

verification.expected: Shows help text for recover command

subtasks.notes: Added Recover variant to Command enum (no arguments). Handler calls reaper::recover_orphans(&running_dir, &pending_dir).await, prints colored output: '✓ recovered: <id> → pending/' for each recovered card, or 'bop recover: nothing to recover.' if none. Command help shows: 'Scan running/ for orphaned cards (dead PIDs, corrupt meta.json) and move them to pending/'. Verified with cargo run -- recover --help.

phases.name: Verification and Documentation
phases.description: Verify all changes work and document the improvements

verification.expected: All tests pass, no clippy warnings, formatting correct

subtasks.notes: All checks passed: 344 unit tests pass (1 ignored), 10 dispatcher harness tests pass, 17 job control tests pass, 4 merge gate tests pass, 5 serve tests pass, 96 bop-core tests pass, 1 doc test pass. Clippy clean with -D warnings flag. Formatting check passes.

verification.instructions: Review result.md for: (1) before/after comparison of write functions, (2) crash scenario analysis showing atomic write prevents corruption, (3) recovery mechanism explanation

subtasks.notes: Comprehensive result.md written covering: (1) before/after comparison of write_meta and write_providers showing fs::write (truncate+write) vs atomic tmp+rename pattern, (2) 4 crash scenarios (power loss during write, crash during rename, orphaned cards, corrupt meta.json) with detailed recovery analysis, (3) recovery mechanisms (recover_orphans algorithm, dispatcher startup integration, bop recover CLI), (4) technical details (POSIX rename atomicity, APFS guarantees, PID liveness via kill(0)), (5) test coverage (344 passing tests), (6) impact analysis (reliability improvements, performance overhead, UX benefits). Document is 419 lines with code examples, test results, and future considerations.

parallel_groups.reason: Subtask 1-1 and 1-2 can run in parallel (independent files), but phase dependencies are sequential

parallelism.speedup_estimate: Sequential execution recommended due to tight coupling

acceptance_criteria: bop recover command exists and works
make check passes (all tests, clippy, fmt)
verification_strategy.reasoning: Medium risk: modifying critical write functions that affect data integrity. Atomic write pattern is well-tested in other systems. Comprehensive test suite should catch regressions.

qa_signoff.notes: All acceptance criteria met. Spec 022 complete and working. Note: Current HEAD (a5817c0) has compilation errors from subsequent spec 023 work, not related to this spec.
